questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P$ be a point on the line segment joining $A(5 \cos \alpha, 5 \sin \alpha)$ and $B(5 \cos \beta, 5 \sin \beta)$ such that $3 P A=2 P B$ then the locus of $P$ is:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } P(h, k) \\ & A(5 \cos \alpha, 5 \sin \alpha) \quad P(h, k) \quad B(5 \cos \beta, 5 \sin \beta) \\ & \quad h=\frac{15 \cos \alpha+10 \cos \beta}{5}, \quad k=\frac{15 \sin \alpha+10 \sin \beta}{5}\end{aligned}$
$h=3 \cos \alpha+2 \cos \beta, \quad k=3 \sin \alpha+2 \sin \beta$
Square and add
$\begin{aligned} & h^2+k^2=13+12 \cos (\alpha-\beta) \\ & x^2+y^2=13+12 \cos (\alpha-\beta)\end{aligned}$
Now, verify.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Locus of the intersection of the two straight lines passing through $(1,0)$ and $(-1,0)$ respectively and including an angle of $45^{\circ}$ can be a circle with
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{m}_1=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{h}-1} ; \mathrm{m}_2=\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{h}+1}$ $\therefore \quad \tan 45^{\circ}=\left|\frac{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{h}-1}-\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{h}+1}}{1+\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{\mathrm{~h}^2-1}}\right|=\left|\frac{\mathrm{k}(\mathrm{h}+1-\mathrm{h}+1)}{\mathrm{h}^2-1+\mathrm{k}^2}\right|$ $h^2+k^2-1= \pm 2 k$
locus is $x^2+y^2 \pm 2 y-1=0$ $x^2+(y \pm 1)^2=(\sqrt{2})^2$ $\Rightarrow(0,1)$ or $(0,-1)$ and radius $=\sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two particles start from the same point $(2,-1)$, one moving 2 units along the line $x+y=1$ and the other 5 units along $x-2 y=4$. If the particles move towards increasing $y$, then their new position will be
questions.solution:
Let $P(2,-1)$ goes 2 units along $x+y=1$ upto $A$ and 5 units along $x-2 y=4$ up to $B$ Slope of $P A=-1=\tan 135^{\circ}$, slope of $P B=\frac{1}{2}=\tan \theta$ $\therefore \sin \theta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}, \cos \theta=\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore A \equiv\left(x_1+r \cos 135^{\circ}, y_1=r \sin 135^{\circ}\right)=\left(2+2 x-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}},-1+2 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=(2-\sqrt{ } 2, \sqrt{ } 2-1) \\ & B \equiv\left(x_1+r \cos \theta, y_1+r \sin \theta\right)=\left(2+5 \times \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}},-1+\frac{5}{\sqrt{5}}\right)=(2 \sqrt{ } 5+2, \sqrt{5}-1)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If the point $(\alpha, 0)$ lies inside the quadrilateral formed by lines $2 x+5 y=15,5 x-4 y=21$, $3 x+5 y+17=0$ and $y=x+3$, then which of the following is true?
options: Number of prime value(s) of $\alpha$ is 4 .
Number of integral value(s) of $\alpha$ is 7.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the straight line $3 x-4 y+7=0$ rotated through $90^{\circ}$ about a point $(3,4)$ meets the coordinate axes at $A$ and $B$ and a $\triangle A O B$ is formed ( $O$ is the origin), then:
options: area of the triangle formed by orthocentre, circumcentre and centroid of the $\triangle A O B$ is 1 sq. units.
area of the triangle formed by orthocentre, circumcentre and incentre of the $\triangle A O B$ is 1 sq. units.
distance between orthocentre and incentre is 2 units.
distance between orthocentre and circumcentre is 5 units.
questions.solution:
Line perpendicular to $3 x-4 y+7=0$ is $4 x+3 y+\lambda=0$, passes $(3,4)$ $4 x+3 y=24$ Orthocenter $(0,0)$ Circumcenter $=$ Mid-point of $A B=(3,4)$ Centroid $\left(2, \frac{8}{3}\right)$ Incenter $(2,2)$
(1) $\quad$ area $(\triangle O C G)=0$ (co-linear point) (2) $\operatorname{area}(\triangle O C I)=1$ (3) $O I=\sqrt{\frac{2}{3}}$ (4) $O C=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\mathrm{A}(3,4) \mathrm{B}(0,0)$ and $C(3,0)$ are vertices of $\Delta A B C$. If ' P ' is a point inside $\triangle A B C$ such that $d(P, B C) \leq \min .\{d(P, A B), d(P, A C)\}$, where $d(P, B C)$ represents distance of $P$ from $B C$, then identify correct statement -
options: if $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC})$ is maximum, then P is incentre of the triangle.
if $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC})$ is maximum, then P is centroid of the triangle.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC}) \leq \min .\{\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{AB}), \mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{AC})\}$ The region represented is shown by shaded region, where BP and CP are angle bisectors Clearly maximum of $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC})$ occurs, when P is incentre of $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ $\therefore \quad$ Maximum of $\mathrm{d}(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{BC})=\mathrm{PN}=$ ordinate of incentre $\mathrm{P}=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The lines $x+y-1=0,(m-1) x+\left(m^2-7\right) y-5=0$ and $(m-2) x+(2 m-5) y=0$ are -
options: concurrent for no value of m
parallel for one value of m
parallel for two values of m
questions.solution:
For concurrency
$\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 1 & -1 \\ m-1 & m^2-7 & -5 \\ m-2 & 2 m-5 & 0\end{array}\right|=0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad 5(2 \mathrm{~m}-5)-5(\mathrm{~m}-2)-[(\mathrm{m}-1)(2 \mathrm{~m}-5) \\
& \left.-(\mathrm{m}-2)\left(\mathrm{m}^2-7\right)\right]=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 10 \mathrm{~m}-25-5 \mathrm{~m}+10-2 \mathrm{~m}^2+7 \mathrm{~m}-5+\mathrm{m}^3 \\
& \quad-2 \mathrm{~m}^2-7 \mathrm{~m}+14=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{~m}^3-4 \mathrm{~m}^2+5 \mathrm{~m}-6=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad(\mathrm{~m}-3)\left(\mathrm{m}^2-\mathrm{m}+2\right)=0
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{m}=3 \text { is the only real root }$
But for $\mathrm{m}=3$ the given line becomes parallel. Thus the given lines can only be parallel for $\mathrm{m}=3$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P$ be any point on the line $x-y+3=0$ and $A$ be a fixed point $(3,4)$. If the family of lines given by the equations $(3 \sec \theta+5 \operatorname{cosec} \theta) x+(7 \sec \theta-3 \operatorname{cosec} \theta) y+11$ $(\sec \theta-\operatorname{cosec} \theta)=0$ are concurrent at a point $B$ for all permissible value of $\theta$, then:
options: sum of the abscissa and ordinate of point $B$ is equal to -1 .
product of the abscissa and ordinate of point $B$ is equal to -2 .
maximum value of $|P A-P B|$ is $2 \sqrt{10}$.
minimum value of $P A+P B$ is $2 \sqrt{34}$.
questions.solution:
$(3 \sec \theta+5 \operatorname{cosec} \theta) x+(7 \sec \theta-3 \operatorname{cosec} \theta) y+11(\sec \theta-\operatorname{cosec} \theta)=0$
$\sec \theta(3 x+7 y+11)+\operatorname{cosec} \theta(5 x-3 y-11)=0$
Hence, family of lines are concurrent at the point of intersection of
$3 x+7 y+11=0 \quad$ and $\quad 5 x-3 y-11=0$
Hence point $B$ is $(1,-2)$. Now proceed.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $a$ is an integer and $m_1, m_2, m_3$ are the slopes of all three straight lines represented by equation $y^3+(2 a+5) x y^2-6 x^2 y-2 a x^3=0$ which are also integers, then which of the following can holds good?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & m_1+m_2+m_3=-(2 a+5) \\ & m_1 m_2+m_2 m_3+m_3 m_1=-6 \\ & m_1 m_2 m_3=2 a \\ & \text { For (A) }\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & a+\sum_{i=1}^3 m_i=-1 \\ & \Rightarrow a-2 a-5=-1 \Rightarrow a=-4 \Rightarrow m_1 m_2 m_3=-8 \\ & \Rightarrow m_1=1, m_2=-2, m_3=4 \Rightarrow m_1 m_2 m_3=-8 \\ & \text { for }(B) \\ & a-2 a-5=-5 \Rightarrow a=0 \Rightarrow m_1 m_2 m_3=0 \\ & \Rightarrow m_1=1, m_2=0, m_3=-6 \\ & \left.\Rightarrow a+\sum_{i=1}^3 m_i=0+0=0 \quad \text { ( } C \text { is correct }\right)\end{aligned}$ for (D) $a+2 a=32$ not possible Hence (D) is incorrect.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two equal sides of an isosceles triangle are given by the equations $7 x-y+3=0$ and $x+y-3=0$ and its third side passes through the point $(1,-10)$. The equation of the third side can be
questions.solution:
$m_{A B}=7 \text { and } m_{A C}=-1$ Let slope of $B C$ (third side) be $m$. Now $\left|\frac{7-\mathrm{m}}{1+7 \mathrm{~m}}\right|=\left|\frac{-1-\mathrm{m}}{1-\mathrm{m}}\right|$ $\Rightarrow\left(\frac{7-\mathrm{m}}{1+7 \mathrm{~m}}\right)= \pm\left(\frac{-1-\mathrm{m}}{1-\mathrm{m}}\right)$
$\therefore$ Taking '+' sign, we get $\mathrm{m}^2+1=0$ (no real values of m possible) And taking '-' sign, we get $3 m^2+8 m-3=0 \Rightarrow(3 m-1)(m+3)=0 \Rightarrow m=\frac{1}{3},-3$ $\therefore \quad$ Required equation of third side can be $x-3 y-31=0$ or $3 x=y+7=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Triangle ABC lies in the Cartesian plane and has an area of 70 sq. units. The coordinates of B and C are $(12,19)$ and $(23,20)$ respecitvely and the coordinates of $A$ are $(p, q)$. The median to the side $B C$ has slope -5 , then which can be corrected.
questions.solution:
$\frac{\left(\frac{39}{2}\right)-\mathrm{q}}{\left(\frac{35}{2}\right)-\mathrm{p}}=-5$ from slope $\begin{aligned} & 39-2 q=-5(35-2 p) \\ & 39-2 q=-175+10 p \\ & \text { i.e., } 5 p+q=107\end{aligned}$ $\pm\left|\begin{array}{ccc}p & q & 1 \\ 12 & 19 & 1 \\ 23 & 20 & 1\end{array}\right|=140 \rightarrow$ From area
i.e. $11 q-p=337,11 q-p=57$ Also, $\underbrace{5 p+q=107}_{\text {solving }} \quad \underbrace{5 p+q=107}_{\text {solving }}$ $\therefore \quad p=15 \& q=32$ $p=20 \& q=7$ So, $p+q=47$ $\mathrm{p}+\mathrm{q}=27$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A B C$ be a triangle such that $A B=A C$. If equation of the side $A B: 7 x+y=0$, $A C: x+y=0$ and line $B C$ is passing through $(2,3)$, then which of the following may be correct?
options: Area of $\triangle A B C$ is $\frac{147}{5}$
Area of $\triangle A B C$ is $\frac{16}{5}$
questions.solution:
Slope of $B C$ is -2 or $\frac{1}{2}$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & B C: 2 x+y=7 \quad \text { or } \quad B C: x-2 y+4=0 \\ & A(0,0), B(7,-7), C\left(\frac{-7}{5}, \frac{49}{5}\right) \quad \Rightarrow \quad \Delta=\frac{147}{5} \\ & A(0,0), B\left(\frac{-4}{3}, \frac{4}{3}\right), C\left(\frac{-4}{15}, \frac{28}{15}\right) \quad \Rightarrow \quad \Delta=\frac{16}{15}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If points of intersection of three non-concurrent lines $x+2 y=3, a x-y=1$ and $x+3 y=5$ lies on a circle and one of the line is diameter of that circle, then:
options: sum of possible values of $a$ is 5
there will be unique value of $a$
$\left(\frac{-1}{7}, \frac{11}{7}\right)$ may be centre of the circle
$\left(\frac{1}{14}, \frac{23}{14}\right)$ may be centre of the circle
questions.solution: (i) If $\frac{-1}{2} \times a=-1 \Rightarrow a=2$ $\therefore \quad$ Centre $=\left(\frac{-1}{7}, \frac{11}{7}\right)$ (ii) If $\frac{-1}{3} \times a=-1 \Rightarrow a=3$ Centre $=\left(\frac{1}{14}, \frac{23}{14}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A B C$ is an equilateral triangle. If the coordinates of the base are $B(1,-1)$ and $C(5,2)$, then the coordinates of vertex $A$ can be
questions.solution:
We have $B C=\sqrt{(5-1)^2+(2+1)^2}=\sqrt{16+9}=5$ Also $m_{B C}=\frac{2-(-1)}{5-1}=\frac{3}{4}$ Midpoint of BC is $\mathrm{M}\left(3, \frac{1}{2}\right)$
Now, equation of AM (in parametric form) is $\frac{x-3}{-\frac{3}{5}}=\frac{y-\frac{1}{2}}{\frac{4}{5}}=r \quad\left(r= \pm \frac{5 \sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ $\therefore \quad \text { We get } \mathrm{x}=3 \mp \frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{2}, \mathrm{y}=\frac{1}{2} \pm 2 \sqrt{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If one vertex of a equilateral triangle of side 2 lies at the origin and other lies on the line $x-\sqrt{3} y=0$, then the coordinates of the third vertex are
questions.solution: The equation of line $x-\sqrt{3} y=0$ in parametric form is $\frac{x-0}{\cos 30^{\circ}}=\frac{y-0}{\sin 30^{\circ}}=r$ $\Rightarrow \frac{x-0}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\frac{y-0}{\frac{1}{2}}=r$ Put $r= \pm 2$, we get $(\sqrt{3}, 1)$ and $(-\sqrt{3},-1)$ Case-1 : Consider two vertices of an equilateral triangle of side 2 as $(0,0)$ and $(\sqrt{3}, 1)$. $\therefore \quad$ Equation of line which is perpendicular bisector of $(0,0)$ and $(\sqrt{3}, 1)$ is $\begin{gathered}\frac{\mathrm{x}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}{\cos 120^{\circ}}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-\frac{1}{2}}{\sin 120^{\circ}}=r_1 \text { (let) } \\ \text { Put } r_1= \pm \sqrt{3}(\text { As altitude }=\sqrt{3})\end{gathered}$ $\therefore \quad x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \pm \sqrt{3}\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right) ; y=\frac{1}{2} \pm \sqrt{3}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ $\Rightarrow x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \mp \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} ; y=\frac{1}{2} \pm \frac{3}{2}$ $\therefore \quad$ Third vertex can be $(0,2)$ and $(\sqrt{3},-1)$ Case-2 : Consider two vertices of an equilateral triangle of side 2 as $(0,0)$ and $(-\sqrt{3},-1)$ $\therefore \quad$ Equation of line which is perpendicular bisector of $(0,0)$ and $(-\sqrt{3},-1)$ is $\frac{x+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}{\cos 120^{\circ}}=\frac{y+\frac{1}{2}}{\sin 120^{\circ}}=r_2$ (let) Put $r_2= \pm \sqrt{3}$ (As altitude $=\sqrt{3}$ ) $\begin{aligned} & \therefore x=\frac{-\sqrt{3}}{2} \pm \sqrt{3}\left(\frac{-1}{2}\right) ; y=\frac{-1}{2} \pm \sqrt{3}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow x=\frac{-\sqrt{3}}{2} \mp \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} ; y=\frac{-1}{2} \pm \frac{3}{2}\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad$ Third vertex can be $(-\sqrt{3}, 1)$ and $(0,-2)$ Note : Number of possible equilateral triangles are four.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $A$ and $B$ are the points $(2,0)$ and $(0,2)$ respectively. The coordinates of the point $P$ on the line $2 x+3 y+1=0$ is/are
questions.solution:
$P$ lies on the line $2 x+3 y+1=0$    ....(1)
$|P A-P B|$ is maximum if $P, A, B$ are collinear.
Equation of the line $A B$ is $x+y=2$    ....(2)
$P$ is the point of intersection of (1) and $(2)$ which is $(7,-5)$
Equation of perpendicular bisector of $A B$ is
$y=x$     .....(3)
For minimum value of $|\mathrm{PA}-\mathrm{PB}|, \mathrm{P}$ is the point of intersection of (1) and (3) which is $\left(-\frac{1}{5},-\frac{1}{5}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
